message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

Debug and resolve a failing dogfight test that's blocking the rbk-09 capture-machinery heat (₣BX). Fresh session —
  CLAUDE.md loads the RBK context for you. This needs live GCP creds + the governor credential on this station.

  Symptom. tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.dogfight.sh runs two cases; rbtdrk_governor_mantle passes, rbtdrk_retriever_invest
  (governor/IAM service-account invest + access-probe) fails. Exact error from the invest step:

  rbgg_invest_retriever ERROR: [rbgg_invest_retriever] Service account
  retriever-canest-ret@cancbhm-d-canest3bhm100001.iam.gserviceaccount.com already exists
  — run the matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) first to re-key

  What it means. The canonical retriever service account was left over from a prior run and never divested. The invest
  preflight in rbgg_Governor.sh (rbgg_invest_retriever) is fail-loud by design when the SA already exists — so this is
  stale GCP account state, not a code regression (lode-lifecycle and the full fast suite are green; the invest path never
  touches the rbk-09 relocation files).

  Where to look.
  - Fixture: Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs — rbtdrk_retriever_invest and the shared rbtdrk_role_invest_impl. A
  comment in role_invest_impl claims a canonical-invest rerun "leaves the invest race-free" — i.e. the fixture is meant
  to be idempotent/self-cleaning. That intended idempotency is what failed.
  - Governor invest: rbgg_invest_retriever in Tools/rbk/rbgg_Governor.sh (the fail-loud preflight).
  - Divest verb: colophon rbw-arD (GovernorDivestsRetriever); the canonical identity is the retriever role.
  - This run's trace (may be cleaned — re-run dogfight to regenerate):
  ../temp-buk/temp-20260604-112141-44242-735/rbtd/rbtdrk_retriever_invest/.

  Resolve, in two layers.
  1. Immediate — divest the leftover SA (GovernorDivestsRetriever / rbw-arD for the canonical retriever identity) so the
  SA is gone.
  2. Root — determine why it was left over: did a prior dogfight run crash before its divest/cleanup, or does the fixture
  lack a pre-divest / self-cleaning step? Make the fixture repeatable — a re-run from a dirty state (leftover SA) must
  pre-divest or be idempotent, per the role_invest_impl intent. Fix the repeatability, don't just clean once.

  Verify. tt/rbw-ts.TestSuite.dogfight.sh green twice in a row (proves idempotency). Surface the root-cause finding
  before changing fixture logic. Natural heat home if you track it as a pace: ₣BY rbk-08-credential-repairs.

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Fix dogfight test retriever service account cleanup

content.text: I'll start plainly: I'm a fresh instance, picking up a debugging task cold. The CLAUDE.md context loaded RBK for me. Before I touch any GCP state, I want to understand the fixture's intended idempotency and the invest preflight, so my "root" fix matches the design intent rather than papering over it. Let me read the key files first.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# shellcheck disable=SC2153  # kindle chain - per BCG
3	#
4	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
5	#
6	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
7	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
8	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
9	#
10	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
11	#
12	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
13	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
14	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
15	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
16	# limitations under the License.
17	#
18	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
19	#
20	# Recipe Bottle GCP Governor - Project Orchestration
21	
22	
23	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
24	# Operational Invariants (RBGG is single writer; 409 is fatal)
25	#
26	# - Single admin actor: All RBGG operations are executed by a single admin
27	#   identity. There are no concurrent writers in the same project.
28	# - Pristine-state expectation: RBGG init/creation flows assume the project
29	#   is pristine for the resources they manage. If a resource "already exists"
30	#   (HTTP 409), that's treated as state drift or prior manual activity.
31	# - Policy: All HTTP 409 Conflict responses are fatal (buc_die). We do not
32	#   treat 409 as idempotent success anywhere in RBGG.
33	#   If you see a 409, resolve state drift first (destroy/reset), then rerun.
34	# ----------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	set -euo pipefail
37	
38	# Multiple inclusion detection
39	test -z "${ZRBGG_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
40	ZRBGG_SOURCED=1
41	
42	######################################################################
43	# Internal Functions (zrbgg_*)
44	
45	zrbgg_kindle() {
46	  test -z "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgg already kindled"
47	
48	  test -n "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}"     || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
49	  test   "${#RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" -gt 0 || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is empty"
50	
51	  buc_log_args 'Ensure dependencies are kindled first'
52	  zrbgc_sentinel
53	  zrbgo_sentinel
54	  zrbuh_sentinel
55	  zrbgi_sentinel
56	
57	  readonly ZRBGG_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_"
58	  readonly ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON="${ZRBGG_PREFIX}empty.json"
59	  printf '{}' > "${ZRBGG_EMPTY_JSON}"
60	
61	  # Infix values for HTTP operations
62	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE="create"
63	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT="preflight"
64	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY="verify"
65	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY="key"
66	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_IAM_ENABLE="api_iam_enable"
67	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CRM_ENABLE="api_crm_enable"
68	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_ART_ENABLE="api_art_enable"
69	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_BUILD_ENABLE="api_build_enable"
70	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_SA_ACCOUNT_GEN="cb_account_gen"
71	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CB_PRIME="cb_prime"
72	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CONTAINERANALYSIS_ENABLE="api_containeranalysis_enable"
73	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_STORAGE_ENABLE="api_storage_enable"
74	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_INFO="project_info"
75	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE_REPO="create_repo"
76	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY_REPO="verify_repo"
77	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_REPO="delete_repo"
78	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_REPO_POLICY="repo_policy"
79	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_RPOLICY_SET="repo_policy_set"
80	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_ROSTER="roster"
81	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK="api_checking"
82	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE="delete"
83	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_GONE="delete_gone"
84	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS="list_keys"
85	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE="bucket_create"
86	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE="bucket_delete"
87	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST="bucket_list"
88	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE="object_delete"
89	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS="list_liens"
90	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE="project_delete"
91	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE="project_state"
92	  readonly ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE="project_restore"
93	
94	  readonly ZRBGG_KINDLED=1
95	}
96	
97	zrbgg_sentinel() {
98	  test "${ZRBGG_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgg not kindled - call zrbgg_kindle first"
99	}
100	
101	# Path-driven sibling of rbuh_json_field_capture for files that live outside
102	# the rbgu cache (under RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/* for credential bytes that
103	# must not leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR).
104	zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture() {
105	  zrbgg_sentinel
106	  local -r z_path="${1}"
107	  local -r z_jq="${2}"
108	  local z_result
109	  z_result=$(jq -r "${z_jq}" "${z_path}")  || return 1
110	  if test -z "${z_result}" || test "${z_result}" = "null"; then return 1; fi
111	  echo "${z_result}"
112	}
113	
114	######################################################################
115	######################################################################
116	# Capture: list required services that are NOT enabled (blank = all enabled)
117	zrbgg_required_apis_missing_capture() {
118	  zrbgg_sentinel
119	
120	  local z_token="${1:-}"
121	  test -n "${z_token}" || { echo ""; return 1; }
122	
123	  local z_missing=""
124	  local z_api=""
125	  local z_service=""
126	  local z_infix=""
127	  local z_state=""
128	  local z_code=""
129	
130	  for z_api in                       \
131	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_CRM}"      \
132	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_GAR}"      \
133	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_IAM}"      \
134	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_BUILD}"    \
135	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_ANALYSIS}" \
136	    "${RBGC_API_SU_VERIFY_STORAGE}"
137	  do
138	    z_service="${z_api##*/}"
139	    z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_API_CHECK}_${z_service}"
140	
141	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_api}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}" || true
142	
143	    buc_log_args 'If we cannot even read an HTTP code file, that is a processing failure.'
144	    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_infix}") || z_code=""
145	    test -n "${z_code}" || return 1
146	
147	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
148	      z_state=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".state") || z_state=""
149	      test "${z_state}" = "ENABLED" || z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
150	    else
151	      buc_log_args 'Any non-200 (403/404/5xx/etc) => treat as NOT enabled'
152	      z_missing="${z_missing} ${z_service}"
153	    fi
154	  done
155	
156	  printf '%s' "${z_missing# }"
157	}
158	
159	zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key() {
160	  zrbgg_sentinel
161	
162	  local z_account_name="$1"
163	  local z_display_name="$2"
164	  local z_description="$3"
165	  local z_role="$4"
166	
167	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
168	
169	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
170	  local z_token
171	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
172	
173	  buc_step "Preflight: confirm ${z_account_name} does not already exist"
174	  # Invest is fail-loud by design (RBSRK/RBSDK): a pre-existing SA is treated as
175	  # state drift, not an idempotent rerun. The operator clears it with the
176	  # matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) before re-investing.
177	  rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" \
178	                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT}"
179	  local z_preflight_code
180	  z_preflight_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PREFLIGHT}") || z_preflight_code=""
181	  case "${z_preflight_code}" in
182	    404) buc_log_args "Service account ${z_account_email} absent — clear to create" ;;
183	    200) buc_die "Service account ${z_account_email} already exists — run the matching GovernorDivests verb (rbw-arD/rbw-adD) first to re-key" ;;
184	    *)   buc_die "Preflight GET for ${z_account_email} returned unexpected HTTP ${z_preflight_code}" ;;
185	  esac
186	
187	  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
188	  jq -n                                      \
189	    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
190	    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
191	    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
192	    '{
193	      accountId: $account_id,
194	      serviceAccount: {
195	        displayName: $display_name,
196	        description: $description
197	      }
198	    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"
199	
200	  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
201	  rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
202	    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
203	  rbuh_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"
204	
205	  local z_sa_uid
206	  z_sa_uid=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
207	    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
208	  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"
209	
210	  rbuh_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
211	    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"
212	
213	  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'
214	
215	  # Subresource read-path can lag SA email-path readiness — flap observed
216	  # 200→200→404 on freshly-minted depots. Retry on the actual call, not a
217	  # proxy probe (see RBSCIP "SA keys subresource propagation").
218	  local z_list_attempt=0
219	  local z_list_infix=""
220	  local z_list_code=""
221	  while :; do
222	    z_list_attempt=$((z_list_attempt + 1))
223	    z_list_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}-attempt${z_list_attempt}"
224	    rbuh_json "GET"                                                        \
225	      "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}"        \
226	                                        "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
227	
228	    z_list_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_list_infix}") || z_list_code=""
229	
230	    if test "${z_list_code}" = "200"; then
231	      break
232	    fi
233	
234	    if test "${z_list_code}" = "404" && test "${z_list_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
235	      buc_warn "keys.list returned 404 (SA read-path propagation delay), retry ${z_list_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
236	      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
237	      continue
238	    fi
239	
240	    rbuh_require_ok "List service account keys" "${z_list_infix}"
241	  done
242	
243	  buc_log_args 'Count existing user-managed keys'
244	  local z_user_keys
245	  z_user_keys=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_list_infix}" \
246	                 '[.keys[]? | select(.keyType=="USER_MANAGED")] | length') \
247	    || buc_die "Failed to parse service account keys"
248	
249	  if test "${z_user_keys}" -gt 0; then
250	    buc_log_args 'Provide a console URL to delete keys manually, then rerun this command'
251	    local z_sa_email_enc="${z_account_email//@/%40}"
252	    local z_keys_url="${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}iam-admin/serviceaccounts/details/${z_sa_email_enc}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
253	
254	    buc_warn "Found ${z_user_keys} existing USER_MANAGED key(s) on ${z_account_email}."
255	    buc_info "Open Console, select the **Keys** tab, delete old keys, then rerun:"
256	    buc_info "  ${z_keys_url}"
257	    buc_die  "Aborting to avoid minting additional keys."
258	  fi
259	
260	  buc_step 'Generate service account key (with propagation retry)'
261	  local z_key_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_key_request.json"
262	  printf '%s' '{"privateKeyType": "TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE"}' > "${z_key_req}"
263	
264	  local z_key_attempt=0
265	  local z_key_infix=""
266	  local z_key_code=""
267	  while :; do
268	    z_key_attempt=$((z_key_attempt + 1))
269	    z_key_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY}-attempt${z_key_attempt}"
270	    rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}" \
271	                                           "${z_token}" "${z_key_infix}" "${z_key_req}"
272	
273	    z_key_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_key_infix}") || z_key_code=""
274	
275	    if test "${z_key_code}" = "200"; then
276	      break
277	    fi
278	
279	    if test "${z_key_code}" = "404" && test "${z_key_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
280	      buc_warn "keys.create returned 404 (SA write-path propagation delay), retry ${z_key_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
281	      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
282	      continue
283	    fi
284	
285	    rbuh_require_ok "Generate service account key" "${z_key_infix}"
286	  done
287	
288	  buc_step 'Extract and decode key data'
289	  local z_key_b64
290	  z_key_b64=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_key_infix}" '.privateKeyData') \
291	    || buc_die "Failed to extract privateKeyData"
292	  # Decode into the assay subdirectory (RBRR_SECRETS_DIR/assay/) so the
293	  # only readable form of the private key shares lifecycle and location
294	  # with the final RBRA file — credentials never leak into BURD_TEMP_DIR.
295	  local -r z_assay_dir="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE%/*}"
296	  local -r z_key_json="${z_assay_dir}/_decoded_key_${z_account_name}.json"
297	  rbgo_base64_decode_string_to_file "${z_key_b64}" "${z_key_json}" \
298	    || buc_die "Failed to decode key data"
299	
300	  buc_step 'Convert JSON key to RBRA format'
301	  local z_rbra_file="${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
302	
303	  local z_client_email
304	  z_client_email=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.client_email') \
305	    || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email from key JSON"
306	
307	  local z_private_key
308	  z_private_key=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.private_key') \
309	    || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key from key JSON"
310	
311	  local z_project_id
312	  z_project_id=$(zrbgg_secrets_json_field_capture "${z_key_json}" '.project_id') \
313	    || buc_die "Failed to extract project_id from key JSON"
314	
315	  buc_step 'Write RBRA file' "${z_rbra_file}"
316	  # CAUTION: jq -r unescapes JSON \n to real newlines, so z_private_key holds a
317	  # multi-line PEM string. printf '%s' below preserves real newlines into the
318	  # RBRA file. Consumer (rbgo_OAuth.sh:zrbgo_build_jwt_capture) tolerates either
319	  # real-newline or '\n'-escape form via printf '%b'; do not "normalize" to one
320	  # form without auditing the consumer.
321	  {
322	    printf 'RBRA_ROLE=%s\n'                  "${z_role}"
323	    printf 'RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="%s"\n'      "${z_client_email}"
324	    printf 'RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="'; printf '%s' "${z_private_key}"; printf '"\n'
325	    printf 'RBRA_PROJECT_ID="%s"\n'        "${z_project_id}"
326	    printf 'RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC=1800\n'
327	  } > "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"
328	
329	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "Failed to write RBRA file ${z_rbra_file}"
330	
331	  # Decoded JSON lives in RBRR_SECRETS_DIR (not BURD_TEMP_DIR), so
332	  # BCG:518's no-module-temp-deletion rule does not bind. Remove now
333	  # that the bytes are persisted in RBRA form.
334	  rm -f "${z_key_json}"
335	
336	  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${z_rbra_file}"
337	
338	  # Echo uniqueId to stdout for callers (all logging goes to stderr per BCG)
339	  printf '%s' "${z_sa_uid}"
340	}

342	zrbgg_create_gcs_bucket() {
343	  zrbgg_sentinel
344	
345	  local z_token="${1}"
346	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
347	
348	  buc_log_args 'Create bucket request JSON for '"${z_bucket_name}"
349	  local z_bucket_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_bucket_create_req.json"
350	  jq -n --arg name "${z_bucket_name}" --arg location "${RBGC_GAR_LOCATION}" '
351	{
352	  name: $name,
353	  location: $location,
354	  storageClass: "STANDARD",
355	  lifecycle: { rule: [ { action: { type: "Delete" }, condition: { age: 1 } } ] }
356	}' > "${z_bucket_req}" || buc_die "Failed to create bucket request JSON"
357	
358	  buc_log_args 'Send bucket creation request'
359	  local z_code
360	  local z_err
361	  rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_GCS_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_token}" \
362	                                  "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" "${z_bucket_req}"
363	  z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}") || buc_die "Bad bucket creation HTTP code"
364	  z_err=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_CREATE}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_code}"
365	
366	  case "${z_code}" in
367	    200|201) buc_info "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} created";                    return 0 ;;
368	    409)     buc_die  "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} already exists (pristine-state violation)" ;;
369	    *)       buc_die  "Failed to create bucket: ${z_err}"                             ;;
370	  esac
371	}

373	zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture() {
374	  zrbgg_sentinel
375	
376	  local z_token="${1}"
377	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
378	
379	  local z_list_url_base="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o"
380	  local z_page_token=""
381	  local z_first=1
382	
383	  while :; do
384	    buc_log_args "Build URL with optional pageToken -> ${z_first}"
385	    local z_url="${z_list_url_base}"
386	    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
387	      buc_log_args 'pageToken must be URL-encoded'
388	      local z_tok_enc
389	      z_tok_enc=$(rbuh_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") || return 1
390	      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
391	    fi
392	
393	    buc_log_args 'Use a unique infix per page to avoid clobbering files'
394	    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_LIST}${z_first}"
395	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"
396	
397	    local z_code
398	    z_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1
399	    test "${z_code}" = "200" || return 1
400	
401	    buc_log_args 'Print names from this page (if any)'
402	    buc_log_args 'Next page?'
403	    jq -r                '.items[]?.name // empty' "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"  || return 1
404	    z_page_token=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.nextPageToken') || z_page_token=""
405	
406	    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
407	    z_first=$((z_first + 1))
408	  done
409	}

427	zrbgg_empty_gcs_bucket() {
428	  zrbgg_sentinel
429	
430	  local z_token="${1}"
431	  local z_bucket_name="${2}"
432	
433	  buc_log_args 'Get list of objects to delete'
434	  local z_objects
435	  z_objects=$(zrbgg_list_bucket_objects_capture "${z_token}" "${z_bucket_name}") || {
436	    buc_log_args 'No objects found or bucket not accessible'
437	    return 0
438	  }
439	
440	  test -n "${z_objects}" || { buc_log_args 'Bucket is empty'; return 0; }
441	
442	  buc_log_args 'Delete each object'
443	  local z_object=""
444	  local z_delete_url=""
445	  local z_delete_code=""
446	  while IFS= read -r z_object; do
447	    test -n "${z_object}" || continue
448	    buc_log_args "Deleting object: ${z_object}"
449	
450	    local z_object_enc
451	    z_object_enc=$(rbuh_urlencode_capture "${z_object}") || z_object_enc=""
452	    test -n "${z_object_enc}" || { buc_warn "Failed to encode object name: ${z_object}"; continue; }
453	    z_delete_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_STORAGE}${RBGC_STORAGE_JSON_V1}/b/${z_bucket_name}/o/${z_object_enc}"
454	
455	    rbuh_json "DELETE" "${z_delete_url}" \
456	                              "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE}"
457	    z_delete_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_OBJECT_DELETE}") || z_delete_code=""
458	    case "${z_delete_code}" in
459	      204|404) buc_log_args "Object ${z_object}: deleted or not found"                     ;;
460	      *)       buc_warn     "Object ${z_object}: Failed to delete (HTTP ${z_delete_code})" ;;
461	    esac
462	  done <<< "${z_objects}"
463	}

481	  z_err=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_BUCKET_DELETE}" '.error.message') || z_err="HTTP ${z_code}"

486	    *)   buc_warn "Bucket ${z_bucket_name} failed delete";                     return 1 ;;
487	  esac

490	######################################################################
491	# External Functions (rbgg_*)
492	
493	# Roster a single role: paginate the SA list, filter by role-prefix,
494	# emit per-identity fact files (basename = identity, content = full email)
495	# and a human-readable line per match. Used by rbgg_roster_retrievers /
496	# rbgg_roster_directors via $1 = role tinder, $2 = fact-extension tinder.
497	zrbgg_roster_role() {
498	  zrbgg_sentinel
499	
500	  local -r z_role="$1"
501	  local -r z_fact_ext="$2"
502	
503	  buc_step "Rostering ${z_role} service accounts in project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
504	
505	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
506	  local z_token
507	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token (rc=$?)"
508	
509	  local z_url_base="${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}"
510	  local z_page_token=""
511	  local z_page=1
512	  local z_count=0
513	
514	  while :; do
515	    local z_url="${z_url_base}"
516	    if test -n "${z_page_token}"; then
517	      local z_tok_enc
518	      z_tok_enc=$(rbuh_urlencode_capture "${z_page_token}") \
519	        || buc_die "Failed to URL-encode pageToken"
520	      z_url="${z_url}?pageToken=${z_tok_enc}"
521	    fi
522	
523	    local z_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_ROSTER}_${z_role}_${z_page}"
524	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_infix}"
525	    rbuh_require_ok "List service accounts (page ${z_page})" "${z_infix}"
526	
527	    local z_sa_count
528	    z_sa_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.accounts // [] | length') \
529	      || buc_die "Failed to parse service accounts page"
530	
531	    local z_index=0
532	    while test "${z_index}" -lt "${z_sa_count}"; do
533	      local z_sa_email
534	      z_sa_email=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" ".accounts[${z_index}].email") \
535	        || { z_index=$((z_index + 1)); continue; }
536	      if [[ "${z_sa_email}" =~ ^${z_role}-(.+)@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}$ ]]; then
537	        local z_identity="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"
538	        buf_write_fact_multi "${z_identity}" "${z_fact_ext}" "${z_sa_email}"
539	        buc_bare "  ${z_identity}  ${z_sa_email}"
540	        z_count=$((z_count + 1))
541	      fi
542	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
543	    done
544	
545	    z_page_token=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${z_infix}" '.nextPageToken') || z_page_token=""
546	    test -n "${z_page_token}" || break
547	    z_page=$((z_page + 1))
548	  done
549	
550	  buc_info "Found ${z_count} ${z_role} service account(s)"
551	  buc_success "Roster operation completed"
552	}

623	rbgg_invest_director() {
624	  zrbgg_sentinel
625	
626	  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
627	
628	  buc_doc_brief "Invest a Director service account for an identity"
629	  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_director}-<identity>"
630	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
631	
632	  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"
633	
634	  local z_account_name="${RBCC_account_director}-${z_identity}"
635	  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
636	
637	  buc_step "Investing Director service account: ${z_account_name}"
638	
639	  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                      \
640	    "${z_account_name}"                                    \
641	    "Recipe Bottle Director (${z_identity})"               \
642	    "Create/destroy container images for ${z_identity}"    \
643	    "${RBCC_role_director}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Director SA"
644	
645	  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
646	  local z_token
647	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
648	
649	  buc_step 'Adding Cloud Build Editor role (project scope)'
650	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
651	    "${z_token}"                            \
652	    "Grant Cloud Build Editor"              \
653	    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
654	    "${RBGC_ROLE_CLOUDBUILD_BUILDS_EDITOR}" \
655	    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
656	    "director-cb"
657	
658	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
659	    "${z_token}"                            \
660	    "Grant Project Viewer"                  \
661	    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
662	    "roles/viewer"                          \
663	    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
664	    "director-viewer"
665	
666	  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
667	    "${z_token}"                            \
668	    "Grant Worker Pool User"               \
669	    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
670	    "roles/cloudbuild.workerPoolUser"       \
671	    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
672	    "director-pool"
673	
674	  buc_step 'Grant serviceAccountUser on Mason'
675	  rbgi_add_sa_iam_role "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "${z_account_email}" "roles/iam.serviceAccountUser"
676	
677	  buc_step 'Grant Artifact Registry roles (complete expected policy)'
678	  # Complete policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer in one setIamPolicy.
679	  # Prevents read-modify-write race where stale getIamPolicy omits Mason's binding.
680	  local -r z_gar_resource="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
681	  local -r z_gar_get_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:getIamPolicy?options.requestedPolicyVersion=3"
682	  local -r z_gar_set_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/${z_gar_resource}:setIamPolicy"
683	
684	  # Propagation retry — AR repo is resource-scope: member-visibility 400s plus
685	  # caller-recently-empowered 403 from the resource-scope IAM cache (the
686	  # governor's roles/owner grant may not yet have reached the AR cache).
687	  local -ra z_gar_tolerance=(
688	    "400" "*does not exist*"
689	    "400" "*is not deleted*"
690	    "403" ""
691	  )
692	  local z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}
693	  local z_gar_prop_elapsed=0
694	  local -r z_gar_prop_deadline=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC}
695	  local z_gar_prop_attempt=0
696	  local z_gar_get_infix=""
697	  local z_gar_set_infix=""
698	  local z_gar_get_code=""
699	
700	  while :; do
701	    z_gar_prop_attempt=$((z_gar_prop_attempt + 1))
702	    z_gar_get_infix="director_gar_get_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
703	    z_gar_set_infix="director_gar_set_iam-${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
704	
705	    rbuh_json "GET" "${z_gar_get_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
706	    z_gar_get_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}") || z_gar_get_code=""
707	
708	    # Propagation retry on GET — covers newly-empowered governor (403) and
709	    # newly-created Director SA member-visibility lag (400 patterns).
710	    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_get_infix}" "${z_gar_get_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
711	      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
712	        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
713	      buc_log_args "GAR getIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_get_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
714	      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
715	      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
716	      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
717	      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
718	      continue
719	    fi
720	
721	    rbuh_require_ok "Get GAR repo IAM policy" "${z_gar_get_infix}"
722	
723	    # Build complete expected policy: Director repoAdmin + Mason writer
724	    local z_gar_partial
725	    z_gar_partial=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "${z_gar_get_infix}" \
726	      "roles/artifactregistry.repoAdmin" "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}" "") \
727	      || buc_die "Failed to add Director repoAdmin to GAR IAM policy"
728	
729	    local z_gar_intermediate="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbuh_director_gar_complete_iam_u_resp.json"
730	    printf '%s\n' "${z_gar_partial}" > "${z_gar_intermediate}" \
731	      || buc_die "Failed to write intermediate GAR IAM policy"
732	
733	    local z_gar_complete
734	    z_gar_complete=$(rbgi_jq_add_member_to_role_capture "director_gar_complete_iam" \
735	      "roles/artifactregistry.writer" "serviceAccount:${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "") \
736	      || buc_die "Failed to add Mason writer to GAR IAM policy"
737	
738	    local z_gar_set_body="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_gar_complete_policy_body.json"
739	    printf '{"policy":%s}\n' "${z_gar_complete}" > "${z_gar_set_body}" \
740	      || buc_die "Failed to write GAR setIamPolicy body"
741	    rbuh_json "POST" "${z_gar_set_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_body}"
742	
743	    local z_gar_set_code
744	    z_gar_set_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${z_gar_set_infix}") || buc_die "No HTTP code from GAR setIamPolicy"
745	
746	    # Propagation retry on SET — same tolerance list as GET.
747	    if zrbgi_propagation_error_predicate "${z_gar_set_infix}" "${z_gar_set_code}" "${z_gar_tolerance[@]}"; then
748	      test "${z_gar_prop_elapsed}" -lt "${z_gar_prop_deadline}" \
749	        || buc_die "GAR IAM: propagation timeout after ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s"
750	      buc_log_args "GAR setIamPolicy returned ${z_gar_set_code} (propagation delay; attempt ${z_gar_prop_attempt}, ${z_gar_prop_elapsed}s)"
751	      sleep "${z_gar_prop_delay}"
752	      z_gar_prop_elapsed=$((z_gar_prop_elapsed + z_gar_prop_delay))
753	      z_gar_prop_delay=$((z_gar_prop_delay * 2))
754	      test "${z_gar_prop_delay}" -le "${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_gar_prop_delay=${RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC}
755	      continue
756	    fi
757	
758	    rbuh_require_ok "Set GAR repo IAM policy (complete)" "${z_gar_set_infix}"
759	    break
760	  done
761	
762	  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
763	  buc_info ""
764	  buc_info "Move the RBRA file to a safe place — typically delivered to the"
765	  buc_info "operator who consumes this identity. When the invester is also the"
766	  buc_info "consumer, the local role slot is the canonical destination:"
767	  buc_bare "        mv ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
768	}
769	
770	# Divest a single role+identity: synthesize email, DELETE (404-tolerant),
771	# opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup only on email match.
772	zrbgg_divest_role() {
773	  zrbgg_sentinel
774	
775	  local -r z_role="$1"
776	  local -r z_identity="$2"
777	  local -r z_role_rbra_file="$3"
778	
779	  local z_account_email="${z_role}-${z_identity}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"
780	
781	  buc_step "Divesting service account: ${z_account_email}"
782	
783	  buc_log_args 'Get OAuth token from admin'
784	  local z_token
785	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
786	
787	  buc_log_args 'Delete via REST API (404-tolerant)'
788	  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" \
789	                                                 "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}"
790	  rbuh_require_ok "Delete service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}" \
791	    404 "not found (already deleted)"
792	
793	  buc_log_args 'Confirm deletion propagated before any same-name recreate'
794	  # When the DELETE actually removed an SA, wait until the GET path reports it
795	  # gone — a delete-accepted response precedes propagation, and an immediately
796	  # following same-name invest would otherwise race a still-visible account.
797	  # Skipped on 404: the account was already absent, nothing to wait for.
798	  local z_delete_code
799	  z_delete_code=$(rbuh_code_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE}") || z_delete_code=""
800	  if test "${z_delete_code}" != "404"; then
801	    rbuh_poll_until_gone "Service account ${z_account_email}" \
802	      "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_DELETE_GONE}"
803	  fi
804	
805	  buc_log_args 'Opportunistic role-RBRA cleanup if installed credential matches'
806	  if test -f "${z_role_rbra_file}"; then
807	    local z_installed_email
808	    z_installed_email=$(. "${z_role_rbra_file}" 2>/dev/null && printf '%s' "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}") \
809	      || z_installed_email=""
810	    if test "${z_installed_email}" = "${z_account_email}"; then
811	      rm -f "${z_role_rbra_file}"
812	      buc_info "Removed installed RBRA at ${z_role_rbra_file} (matched divested email)"
813	    fi
814	  fi
815	
816	  buc_success "Divest operation completed"
817	}
818	
819	rbgg_divest_retriever() {
820	  zrbgg_sentinel
821	
822	  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
823	
824	  buc_doc_brief "Divest a Retriever service account by identity"
825	  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_retriever}-<identity>"
826	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
827	
828	  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"
829	
830	  zrbgg_divest_role "${RBCC_account_retriever}" "${z_identity}" "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
831	}
832	
833	rbgg_divest_director() {
834	  zrbgg_sentinel
835	
836	  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
837	
838	  buc_doc_brief "Divest a Director service account by identity"
839	  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_account_director}-<identity>"
840	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
841	
842	  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"
843	
844	  zrbgg_divest_role "${RBCC_account_director}" "${z_identity}" "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
845	}
846	
847	rbgg_destroy_project() {
848	  zrbgg_sentinel
849	  buc_doc_brief "DEPRECATED: Use rbgp_project_delete instead - moved to Payor module for billing/destructive ops"
850	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
851	
852	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
853	  buc_warn "DEPRECATION NOTICE: rbgg_destroy_project is deprecated"
854	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
855	  buc_warn ""
856	  buc_warn "Project deletion has been moved to the Payor module which handles"
857	  buc_warn "all billing and destructive lifecycle operations."
858	  buc_warn ""
859	  buc_warn "Use instead:"
860	  buc_warn "  rbgp_project_delete - Full project deletion with proper safeguards"
861	  buc_warn ""
862	  buc_warn "The Payor module provides additional features like lien management,"
863	  buc_warn "billing detachment, and project restoration capabilities."
864	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
865	
866	  buc_die "Function moved to Payor module - use rbgp_project_delete"
867	
868	  if [[ "${DEBUG_ONLY:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
869	    buc_die "This dangerous operation requires DEBUG_ONLY=1 environment variable"
870	  fi
871	
872	  buc_step 'Mint admin OAuth token'
873	  local z_token
874	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
875	
876	  buc_step 'Triple confirmation required'
877	  buc_warn ""
878	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
879	  buc_warn "CRITICAL WARNING: You are about to PERMANENTLY DELETE the entire project:"
880	  buc_warn "  Project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
881	  buc_warn "This will:"
882	  buc_warn "  - Delete ALL resources in the project"
883	  buc_warn "  - Delete ALL data permanently"
884	  buc_warn "  - Break billing associations"
885	  buc_warn "  - Make the project unusable immediately"
886	  buc_warn "  - Cannot be undone after 30-day grace period"
887	  buc_warn "========================================================================"
888	  buc_warn ""
889	
890	  buc_require "Type the exact project ID to confirm deletion" "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
891	  buc_require "Confirm you understand this DELETES EVERYTHING in the project" "DELETE-EVERYTHING"
892	  buc_require "Final confirmation - type OBLITERATE to proceed" "OBLITERATE"
893	
894	  buc_step 'Check for liens (will block deletion)'
895	  rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/liens?parent=projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
896	  rbuh_require_ok "List liens" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}"
897	
898	  local z_lien_count
899	  z_lien_count=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_LIENS}" '.liens // [] | length') || buc_die "Failed to parse liens response"
900	
901	  if [[ "${z_lien_count}" -gt 0 ]]; then
902	    buc_step 'BLOCKED: Liens exist on project'
903	    buc_warn "Project has ${z_lien_count} lien(s) that prevent deletion"
904	    buc_warn "You must remove all liens first:"
905	    buc_code "  gcloud resource-manager liens list --project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
906	    buc_code "  gcloud resource-manager liens delete LIEN_NAME --project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
907	    buc_warn "Then re-run this command."
908	    buc_die "Cannot proceed with active liens"
909	  fi
910	
911	  buc_step 'Delete project (immediate lifecycle change to DELETE_REQUESTED)'
912	  rbuh_json "DELETE" "${RBGD_API_CRM_DELETE_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE}"
913	  rbuh_require_ok "Delete project" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_DELETE}"
914	
915	  buc_step 'Verify deletion state'
916	  rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
917	  rbuh_require_ok "Get project state" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
918	
919	  local z_lifecycle_state
920	  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
921	
922	  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
923	    buc_success "Project successfully marked for deletion"
924	    buc_step "Project Status: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
925	    buc_step "Grace period: Up to 30 days"
926	    buc_code "To restore (if still possible): rbgg_restore_project"
927	    buc_step "WARNING: Project is now unusable but may remain visible in listings"
928	  else
929	    buc_die "Unexpected project state after deletion: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
930	  fi
931	}
932	
933	rbgg_restore_project() {
934	  zrbgg_sentinel
935	  buc_doc_brief "Attempt to restore a deleted project within the 30-day grace period"
936	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
937	
938	  buc_step 'Mint admin OAuth token'
939	  local z_token
940	  z_token=$(rba_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"
941	
942	  buc_step 'Check current project state'
943	  rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
944	
945	  if ! rbuh_code_ok_predicate "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"; then
946	    buc_die "Cannot access project - it may have been permanently deleted or never existed"
947	  fi
948	
949	  local z_lifecycle_state
950	  z_lifecycle_state=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse project state"
951	
952	  if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" != "DELETE_REQUESTED"; then
953	    buc_die "Project state is ${z_lifecycle_state} - can only restore projects in DELETE_REQUESTED state"
954	  fi
955	
956	  buc_step 'Confirm restoration'
957	  buc_log_args "Project Status: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
958	  buc_log_args "Attempting to restore project: ${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"
959	  buc_log_args "WARNING: Restore may fail if deletion process has already started"
960	  buc_require "Confirm restoration of project" "RESTORE"
961	
962	  buc_step 'Attempt project restoration'
963	  rbuh_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_CRM_UNDELETE_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE}"
964	  if rbuh_code_ok_predicate                                                    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE}"; then
965	    buc_step 'Verify restoration'
966	    rbuh_json "GET" "${RBGD_API_CRM_GET_PROJECT}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
967	    rbuh_require_ok "Get restored project state"               "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}"
968	
969	    z_lifecycle_state=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_STATE}" '.lifecycleState // "UNKNOWN"') || buc_die "Failed to parse restored project state"
970	
971	    if test "${z_lifecycle_state}" = "ACTIVE"; then
972	      buc_success "Project successfully restored to ACTIVE state"
973	      buc_log_args "Project Status: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
974	      buc_log_args "Project is now usable again"
975	    else
976	      buc_die "Restoration completed but project state is unexpected: ${z_lifecycle_state}"
977	    fi
978	  else
979	    local z_error_msg
980	    z_error_msg=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_PROJECT_RESTORE}" '.error.message // "Unknown error"') || z_error_msg="Failed to parse error"
981	    buc_die "Project restoration failed: ${z_error_msg}"
982	  fi
983	}
984	
985	# eof
986	
987
